name: Chocolate-Almond Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Buttermilk (or milk + vinegar, mixed until curdled)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine cocoa and boiling water in a bowl; stir until smooth. Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, beat shortening with an electric mixer on medium speed until creamy.
3. Gradually add sugar, beating well after each addition.
4. Add eggs one at a time, mixing well after each egg is incorporated.
5. Combine flour, baking soda, and salt; stir into the creamed mixture alternately with buttermilk, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Mix just until blended after each addition.
6. Stir in the chocolate water mixture and vanilla extract.
7. Divide batter evenly among three greased and floured 8-inch round cake pans.
8. Bake at 350°F for 20 to 25 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted into the center comes out clean.
9. Cool in the pans for 10 minutes, then remove from pans and let cool completely on wire racks.
10. Spread almond cream filling between layers. Reserve some frosting for later use.
11. Spread remaining frosting over the top and sides of the cake.
12. Pipe or dollop reserved frosting onto the top of the cake and sprinkle with toasted almonds.
Yield: One 3-layer cake
Filling Yield: Serves as filling for the entire cake
Frosting Yield: Enough to frost one 3-layer cake
